title: Sarga 37
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “When Rama, whose soul was known for righteousness, had been consecrated, the first night, which increased the joy of the citizens, passed.” and develops toward “Thousands of humble men, with folded hands, approached the risen great-souled Rama, offering him water in pure vessels.”

This passage begins with “Having purified himself with ablutions and offered oblations to the sacred fire at the appropriate time, he quickly went to the holy temple, which was traditionally frequented by the…” and develops toward “To those who were seated, various very sweet stories imbued with righteousness were narrated by great souls who were learned in the Puranas.…”

verses.sanskrit: अभिषिक्ते तु काकुत्स्थे धर्मेण विदितात्मनि । व्यतीता या निशा पूर्वा पौराणां हर्षवर्धिनी ।। 7.37.1 ।।
verses.translation: When Rama, whose soul was known for righteousness, had been consecrated, the first night, which increased the joy of the citizens, passed.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यां रजन्यां व्युष्टायां प्रातर्नृपतिबोधकाः । वन्दिनः समुपातिष्ठन्सौम्या नृपतिवेश्मनि ।। 7.37.2 ।।
verses.translation: As that night dawned, in the morning, gentle bards, who were the awakeners of the king, stood near the king's palace.

verses.sanskrit: ते रक्तकण्ठिनः सर्वे किन्नरा इव शिक्षिताः । तुष्टुवुर्नपतिं वीरं यथावत् सम्प्रहर्षिणः ।। 7.37.3 ।।
verses.translation: All of them, with melodious voices, trained like Kinnaras, greatly rejoicing, duly praised the heroic king.

verses.sanskrit: वीर सौम्य प्रबुध्यस्व कौसल्याप्रीतिवर्धन । जगद्धि सर्वं स्वपिति त्वयि सुप्ते नराधिप ।। 7.37.4 ।।
verses.translation: O heroic and gentle king, who increases Kausalya's joy, please awaken, for indeed, when you sleep, the entire world sleeps.

verses.sanskrit: विक्रमस्ते यथा विष्णो रूपं चैवाश्विनोरिव । बुद्ध्या बृहस्पतेस्तुल्यः प्रजापतिसमो ह्यसि ।। 7.37.5 ।।
verses.translation: Your valor is like that of Vishnu, your beauty is like that of the Ashvins, you are equal to Brihaspati in intellect, and indeed, you are like Prajapati himself.

verses.sanskrit: क्षमा ते पृथिवीतुल्या तेजसा भास्करोपमः । वेगस्ते वायुना तुल्यो गाम्भीर्यमुदधेरिव ।। 7.37.6 ।।
verses.translation: Your patience is like that of the earth, your splendor is comparable to the sun, your speed is equal to the wind, and your depth is like that of the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: अप्रकम्प्यो यता स्थाणुश्चन्द्रे सौम्यत्वमीदृशम् । नेदृशाः पार्थिवाः पूर्वं भवितारो नराधिप ।। 7.37.7 ।।
verses.translation: You are unshakable like a pillar, and possess such gentleness as found in the moon; O king of men, there have been no such kings before, nor will there be in the future.

verses.sanskrit: यथा त्वमतिदुर्धर्षो धर्मनित्यः प्रजाहितः । न त्वां जहाति कीर्तिश्च लक्ष्मीश्च पुरुषर्षभ ।। 7.37.8 ।।
verses.translation: As you are extremely formidable, always righteous, and devoted to the welfare of your subjects, O best among men, neither fame nor prosperity ever abandons you.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीश्च धर्मश्च काकुत्स्थ त्वयि नित्यं प्रतिष्ठितौ । एताश्चान्याश्च मधुरा वन्दिभिः परिकीर्तिताः ।। 7.37.9 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, prosperity and righteousness are eternally established in you, and these and other sweet praises were sung by the bards.

verses.sanskrit: सूताश्च संस्तवैर्दिव्यैर्बोधयन्ति स्म राघवम् । स्तुतिभिः स्तूयमानाभिः प्रत्यबुध्यत राघवः ।। 7.37.10 ।।
verses.translation: The bards, with their divine praises, awakened Rama, and Rama awoke to the sound of these eulogies being sung.

verses.sanskrit: स तद्विहाय शयनं पाण्डराच्छादनास्तृतम् । उत्तस्थौ नागशयनाद्धरिर्नारायणो यथा ।। 7.37.11 ।।
verses.translation: He rose from that bed, which was covered with white sheets, just as Lord Narayana (Vishnu) rises from his serpent-bed.

verses.sanskrit: तमुत्थितं महात्मानं प्रह्वाः प्राञ्जलयो नराः । सलिलं भाजनैः शुभ्रैरुपतस्थुः सहस्रशः ।। 7.37.12 ।।
verses.translation: Thousands of humble men, with folded hands, approached the risen great-souled Rama, offering him water in pure vessels.

verses.sanskrit: कृतोदकशुचिर्भूत्वा काले हुतहुताशनः । देवागारं जगामाशु पुण्यमिक्ष्वाकुसेवितम् ।। 7.37.13 ।।
verses.translation: Having purified himself with ablutions and offered oblations to the sacred fire at the appropriate time, he quickly went to the holy temple, which was traditionally frequented by the Ikshvaku kings.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र देवान्पितऽन्विप्रानर्चयित्वा यथाविधि । बाह्यकक्षान्तरं रामो निर्जगाम जनैर्वृतः ।। 7.37.14 ।।
verses.translation: After duly worshipping the gods, ancestors, and Brahmins, Rama emerged into the outer chamber, surrounded by many people.

verses.sanskrit: उपतस्थुर्महात्मानो मन्त्रिणः सपुरोहिताः । वसिष्ठप्रमुखाः सर्वे दीप्यमाना इवाग्नयः ।। 7.37.15 ।।
verses.translation: All the great-souled ministers, along with their priests and headed by Vasishtha, approached Rama, shining brightly like fires.

verses.sanskrit: क्षत्रियाश्च महात्मानो नानाजनपदेश्वराः । रामस्योपाविशन्पार्श्वे शक्रस्येव यथा ऽमराः ।। 7.37.16 ।।
verses.translation: Great-souled Kshatriyas and rulers from various regions sat by Rama's side, just as the immortals sit by Indra.

verses.sanskrit: भरतो लक्ष्मणश्चात्र शत्रुघ्नश्च महायशाः । उपासाञ्चक्रिरे हृष्टा वेदास्त्रय इवाध्वरम् ।। 7.37.17 ।।
verses.translation: There, the highly renowned Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna attended upon Rama with delight, just as the three Vedas attend upon a sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: याताः प्राञ्जलयो भूत्वा किङ्करा मुदिताननाः । मुदिता नाम पार्श्वस्था बहवः समुपाविशन् ।। 7.37.18 ।।
verses.translation: Many servants with joyful faces came with folded hands and, indeed joyful, sat down by his side.

verses.sanskrit: वानराश्च महावीर्या विंशतिः कामरूपिणः । सुग्रीवप्रमुखा राममुपासन्ते महौजसः ।। 7.37.19 ।।
verses.translation: Twenty greatly valorous and powerful monkeys, led by Sugriva, who can assume any form at will, serve Rama.

verses.sanskrit: विभीषणश्च रक्षोभिश्चतुर्भिः परिवारितः । उपासते महात्मानं धनेशमिव गुह्यकाः ।। 7.37.20 ।।
verses.translation: And Vibhishana, surrounded by four rakshasas, serves the great-souled Rama, just as Guhyakas serve Kubera, the lord of wealth.

verses.sanskrit: तथा निगमवृद्धाश्च कुलीना ये च मानवाः । शिरसा ऽ ऽवन्द्य राजानमुपासन्ते विचक्षणाः ।। 7.37.21 ।।
verses.translation: Similarly, the wise elders of the guilds and noble-born men serve the king, Rama, having bowed down with their heads.

verses.sanskrit: तथा परिवृतो राजा श्रीमद्भिर्ऋषिभिर्वृतः । राजभिश्च महावीर्यैर्वानरैश्च सराक्षसैः ।। 7.37.22 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, the king Rama is surrounded by glorious sages, greatly valorous kings, monkeys, and rakshasas.

verses.sanskrit: यथा देवेश्वरो नित्यमृषिभिः समुपास्यते । अधिकस्तेन रूपेण सहस्राक्षाद्विरोचते ।। 7.37.23 ।।
verses.translation: Just as the lord of gods, Indra, is always served by sages, Rama shines with a splendor superior to that of the thousand-eyed Indra.

verses.sanskrit: तेषां समुपविष्टानां तास्ताः सुमधुराः कथाः । कथ्यन्ते धर्मसंयुक्ताः पुराणज्ञैर्महात्मभिः ।। 7.37.24 ।।
verses.translation: To those who were seated, various very sweet stories imbued with righteousness were narrated by great souls who were learned in the Puranas. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे सप्तत्रिंशः सर्गः ।। 37 ।। Thus ends the thirty-seventh sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
